# feat(nexus): SPEC-06 PACTMiddleware governance enforcement

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `2cb3fb66ff28` — feat(nexus): SPEC-06 PACTMiddleware governance enforcement

**Body**:

Adds packages/kailash-nexus/src/nexus/middleware/governance.py with
PACTMiddleware class implementing the operating-envelope enforcement
point. Wires PACT governance into Nexus request path between authN
(Nexus) and business logic.

PACTMiddleware intercepts every non-exempt HTTP request, extracts the
agent role_address from scope state or X-PACT-Role-Address header,
derives a structural action identifier from method + first path segment
(no semantic/content routing per agent-reasoning.md MUST Rule 5), and
calls GovernanceEngine.verify_action() to get a verdict. Fail-closed on
any exception, missing engine, missing role_address, or BLOCKED verdict
(403). HELD verdicts return 429. Structured logs emit at entry, envelope
resolution, decision, and exit with correlation_id propagation per
observability.md. NaN/Inf costs in scope state are rejected before
reaching the engine per pact-governance.md Rule 6.

NexusEngine.builder() gains a .governance(engine) method that registers
PACTMiddleware into the underlying Nexus middleware stack after the
preset's auth chain is constructed, placing PACT authZ INSIDE the Nexus
authN wrapper (authN -> authZ -> handler ordering).

Tier 1 unit tests (29 cases) cover construction fail-closed, action
derivation, scope type gating, exempt paths, role-address resolution
priority, verdict handling (auto_approved/flagged/blocked/held),
exception-to-403 path, correlation-id echo, and NaN/Inf cost defense.

Tier 2 integration tests (8 cases) drive a real Nexus() + FastAPI app +
real kailash.trust.pact.GovernanceEngine via httpx.ASGITransport, verify
allow/deny matrix on a minimal 2-role org, exempt-path bypass,
correlation id round-trip, structured log emission, and the
NexusEngine.builder().governance(engine).build() wiring path.

No new dependencies — kailash-nexus already depends on kailash (which
carries kailash.trust.pact) and starlette.

Fixes spec-compliance v2 CRITICAL #6.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
